metric_value: 'Given the global economic uncertainty, Intel has executed with some dexterity and deserves a lot of credit for their numbers.' — Peter Kastner, Chief Research Officer, Aberdeen Group, following Intel's Q1 2003 earnings release
Intel reported first-quarter revenue of $6.75B (down 6% sequentially, flat YoY) on April 15 2003; stock rose nearly $2 that week to end at $18.66
Apple posted 65% YoY earnings decline for quarter reported that week; denied rumors of Universal Music bid
President Bush labeled his 'jobs and growth' proposal as 'urgent in January' and 'even more urgent now'; sought $550B tax cut
